title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover NZ school holidays travel tips to save money and avoid crowds, including peak vs off-peak pricing and booking strategies
intro: Travelling during NZ school holidays can be expensive and chaotic, with popular destinations like Queenstown and Rotorua often booked out months in advance. However, with some strategic planning, you can beat the crowds and save money. For example, considering a trip to Wellington instead of Auckland can save you around $200 on accommodation per night.

sections.heading: Understanding Peak vs Off-Peak Pricing
sections.body: NZ school holidays typically occur in April, July, September, and December, with prices for accommodation and activities increasing significantly during these periods. For instance, a family room at the Novotel Wellington can cost around $350 per night during peak season, compared to $250 per night during off-peak season.

sections.heading: Booking Lead Times
list: Book your accommodation at least 3-4 months in advance to secure the best prices
Consider booking activities and tours 1-2 months in advance to avoid sold-out situations
Be flexible with your travel dates to take advantage of off-peak pricing and shorter drive times, such as visiting the Coromandel Peninsula instead of the busy Lake Wakatipu

sections.heading: Driving Times and Distances
sections.body: When planning your road trip, consider the driving times and distances between destinations. For example, driving from Auckland to Rotorua takes around 3 hours, while driving from Wellington to Queenstown takes around 10 hours with a ferry crossing. You can use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to estimate your fuel costs and plan your itinerary.

sections.heading: Saving Money on Accommodation
list: Consider staying in a campervan or holiday park to save around $100-200 per night
Look for discounted rates or package deals on booking websites like Booking.com or Expedia
Book a self-catering apartment to save money on food and laundry, such as a 2-bedroom apartment in Wellington for around $250 per night

sections.heading: Get Ready for Your Next Family Adventure

cta.text: Discover more family-friendly destinations and activities in NZ
